findings.context: "But I was established as King by Him Over His holy hill of Zion, Declaring the Lord's decree.
You shall shepherd them with an iron staff; You shall shatter them like a potter's vessel.' "
thus he said: "I will love You, O Lord, my strength.
He who magnifies the salvation of His king And shows mercy to His anointed, To David and his seed forever."
"I will give you understanding, and I will teach you in the way you should walk; I will fix My eyes on you.
Do not be like the horse and the mule, which have no understanding; You squeeze their jaws with bit and bridle, Lest they come near you."
"O Lord, make me to know my end, And what is the measure of my days, So as to know what I lack.
"Nevertheless man walks about like a phantom; Surely in vain they stir themselves up; He stores up treasure, but does not know for whom he will gather it.
"Hear my prayer, O Lord, And give ear to my supplication; Do not be silent at my tears, For I am a sojourner before You, And a stranger, as were all my fathers.
Do not forsake me, that I may revive Before I depart and am no longer here."
Many, O Lord my God, are the wonders You performed, And in Your thoughts there is no one who shall be likened to You; I declared and said, "They are multiplied beyond number.
Sacrifice and offering You did not will; But a body You prepared for me; A whole burnt offering and a sin offering You did not require."
Then I said, "Behold, I come (it is written of me in the volume of the book);
I willed to do Your will, O my God, And Your law in the midst of my heart."
To the sinner God says, "Why do you declare My ordinances, And take up My covenant in your mouth?
A sacrifice of praise shall glorify Me, And there is the way whereby I will show him the salvation of God."
I said, "Who will give me wings like a dove, And I will fly away, and be at rest?
Behold, I fled far away, And lodged in the wilderness." (Pause)
Say to God, "How fearful are Your works; In the greatness of Your power Your enemies will lie to You;
Let all the earth worship You and sing to You; Let them sing to Your name." (Pause)
The Lord said, "I will return from Bashan; I will return in the depths of the sea,
So your foot may be dipped in blood, The tongue of your dogs in the blood of your enemies."
I shall describe all Your wonders. "When I seize the opportunity, I shall judge these rightly.
The earth is dissolved and all its inhabitants; I made firmits pillars." (Pause)
I said to those who transgress the law, "Do not transgress the law," And to those who sin I said, "Do not exalt yourselves;
Do not boast in yourselves; Do not accuse God of wrongdoing."
And they slandered God and said, "Why is God unable to prepare a table in the desert?
Since He struck the rock, and waters flowed, And brooks flooded, Is He also unable to give us bread Or to prepare a table for His people?"
I said, "You are gods, And you are all sons of the Most High.
But you die like men, And like one of the rulers, you fall."
For You said, "Mercy shall be built up forever; Your truth shall be prepared in the heavens.
I shall prepare your seed forever, And I shall build your throne from generation to generation." (Pause)
Then You spoke to Your holy ones in a vision, And You said, "I established help for a mighty one; I raised up a chosen one from My people;
And as the moon that is established forever And is a faithful witness in heaven." (Pause)
He shall say to the Lord, "You are my protector and my refuge, My God; I will hope in Him;
For He shall free me from the snare of the hunters, And from every troubling word."
"For he hoped in Me, and I will deliver him; I will shelter him, because he knew My name.
With length of days I will satisfy him, And show him My salvation."
"Do not harden your hearts as in the Rebellion, During the day of testing in the desert,
So I swore in My wrath, 'They shall not enter My rest.' "
He replied to Him in the way of strength, "Declare to me the fewness of my days;
The children of Your servants shall dwell there, And their seed shall be led to prosperity forever."
God spoke in His place, "I shall be exalted, and I will divide Shechem, And measure out the valley of tents;
Moab is the cauldron of My hope; I will stretch out My shoe over Edom; Foreign tribes were subjected to Me."
"If the Lord had not been with us, When men rose up against us,
Then our soul would have passed through A water that is overwhelming."
"Many times they warred against me from my youth, Yet they did not overcome me.
The sinners schemed behind my back; They prolonged their lawlessness."
"I shall not enter my dwelling, I shall not recline on my bed,
Until I find a place for the Lord, A tabernacle for the God of Jacob."
The Lord swore to David this truth, and He shall not reject it, "I shall set upon your throne one from the fruit of your loins.
If your sons keep My covenant And these testimonies I shall teach them, Then their sons shall sit upon your throne forever."
"This is My place of rest unto ages of ages; Here I shall dwell, for I have chosen her.
His enemies I shall clothe with shame; Upon him shall My sanctification flourish."
I said to the Lord, "You are my God; Give ear, O Lord, to the voice of my supplication.
"As for the head of those who encircle me, The toil of their lips shall cover them.
Coals shall fall upon them; You shall overthrow them with fire; Do not let them withstand hardships."
I cried to You, O Lord; I said, "You are my hope, My portion in the land of the living.
Bring my soul out of prison To give thanks to Your name, O Lord; The righteous shall wait for me, until You reward me."
"Come with us; take part in bloodshed, And let us hide a righteous man in the earth unjustly;
Cast your lot with us And let us acquire a common bag; Let there be one purse for us."
"So long a time as the simple hold fast to righteousness, They shall not be ashamed;
But he who obeys me shall dwell in hope, And shall be at rest without fear from every evil."
Who taught me, saying, "Let our word become firmly planted in your heart;
And give your head a crown of graces, And cover you with a crown of delight."
And you will say, "How I hated instruction And turned my heart away from reproofs;
Little by little I was in every evil In the midst of the church and congregation."
"I have a peace offering; Today I pay my vows;
Taking a bag of money in his hand; He will return to his house after many days."
"You, O men, I exhort, And I utter my voice to the sons of men;
But those who sin against me act ungodly toward their own souls, And those who hate me love death."
"Come, eat my bread And drink the wine I mixed for you;
Forsake lack of discernment, and you shall live; Seek discernment so you may live, And keep straight your understanding with knowledge."
"He who is very lacking in discernment, let him turn aside to me; And he who is in need of discernment, I exhort, saying,
'Grasp with pleasure the secret bread And the sweet water of theft.' "
For they said among themselves, as they reasoned incorrectly: "Our life is short and painful, And there is no cure for the death of a man; For no one has been known to return from Hades;
